title: अयप्पा अष्टकम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री अयप्पा अष्टकम् ॥

॥ ध्यानम् ॥
ध्यायेत् सततं महिषीसुतं शिवहरिप्रेमस्वरूपं विभुम्।
नीलाम्बरधरं प्रसन्नवदनं शास्तारमाद्यं गुरुम्॥

शिवपुत्रं हरिसुतं धर्मशास्तारमव्ययम्।
मणिकण्ठं महावीरं अयप्पं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ १॥

शबरीगिरिवासं तं पम्पातटनिवासिनम्।
भक्तकल्पतरुं देवं अयप्पं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ २॥

महिषीसुरसंहारं धर्मरक्षणतत्परम्।
योगमायाविलासं तं अयप्पं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ३॥

पन्थलराजपालितं वनवासरतं सदा।
मण्डलव्रतपूजार्हं अयप्पं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ४॥

नीलिमलनिवासं तं तत्त्वमस्यार्थबोधकम्।
ब्रह्मचर्यव्रतस्थं तं अयप्पं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ५॥

पतिनैट्टाम्पडिदेवं एरुमेलिमहोत्सवम्।
मकरज्योतिदर्शं तं अयप्पं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ६॥

शरणमन्त्रमयं देवं स्वामिये शरणमित्यपि।
भक्तसंघसमन्वितं अयप्पं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ७॥

हरिहरात्मजं देवं सर्वपापविनाशनम्।
सर्वलोकहिते रतं अयप्पं प्रणमाम्यहम्॥ ८॥

॥ फलश्रुति ॥
अयप्पाष्टकमिदं पुण्यं यः पठेत् भक्तिभावतः।
शास्तुः प्रसादमाप्नोति सर्वसिद्धिं च विन्दति॥

मण्डलव्रतकाले तु पठनात् सर्वसम्पदः।
शबरीगिरिदर्शं तु लभते नात्र संशयः॥

॥ इति श्री अयप्पा अष्टकं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Ayyappa Ashtakam ||

|| Dhyanam ||
Dhyayet Satatam Mahishi-sutam Shiva-hari-prema-svarupam Vibhum.
Nila-ambara-dharam Prasanna-vadanam Shastaram-aadyam Gurum.

Shiva-putram Hari-sutam Dharma-shastaram-avyayam.
Mani-kantham Maha-viram Ayyappam Pranamamyaham. || 1 ||

Shabari-giri-vasam Tam Pampa-tata-nivasinam.
Bhakta-kalpa-tarum Devam Ayyappam Pranamamyaham. || 2 ||

Mahishi-sura-samharam Dharma-rakshana-tatparam.
Yoga-maya-vilasam Tam Ayyappam Pranamamyaham. || 3 ||

Panthala-raja-palitam Vana-vasa-ratam Sada.
Mandala-vrata-pujarham Ayyappam Pranamamyaham. || 4 ||

Nilimala-nivasam Tam Tattvam-asi-artha-bodhakam.
Brahmacharya-vrata-stham Tam Ayyappam Pranamamyaham. || 5 ||

Pati-naittam-padi-devam Erumeli-mahotsavam.
Makara-jyoti-darsham Tam Ayyappam Pranamamyaham. || 6 ||

Sharana-mantra-mayam Devam Svamiye Sharanam-ityapi.
Bhakta-sangha-samanvitam Ayyappam Pranamamyaham. || 7 ||

Hari-hara-atmajam Devam Sarva-papa-vinashanam.
Sarva-loka-hite Ratam Ayyappam Pranamamyaham. || 8 ||

|| Phalashruti ||
Ayyappa-ashtakam-idam Punyam Yah Pathet Bhakti-bhavatah.
Shastuh Prasadam-aapnoti Sarva-siddhim Cha Vindati.

Mandala-vrata-kale Tu Pathanat Sarva-sampadah.
Shabari-giri-darsham Tu Labhate Natra Samshayah.

|| Iti Shri Ayyappa Ashtakam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
शिवपुत्र, हरिसुत, धर्मशास्ता, अव्यय, मणिकण्ठ, महावीर श्री अयप्पा स्वामी को प्रणाम। शबरीगिरि (सबरीमाला) में निवास करने वाले, पम्पा तट पर विराजमान, भक्तों के कल्पतरु स्वामी अयप्पा को प्रणाम।

महिषासुर का संहार करने वाले, धर्मरक्षा में तत्पर, योगमाया के विलास स्वरूप! पन्थलराज के द्वारा पालित, वनवास में रत, मण्डल व्रत से पूजनीय!

नीलिमल (नीलगिरि) में निवास करने वाले, 'तत्त्वमसि' के अर्थ बोधक, ब्रह्मचर्य व्रत में स्थित! पतिनैट्टाम्पडि के देव, एरुमेली महोत्सव के स्वामी, मकरज्योति दर्शन दाता!

'स्वामिये शरणम्' मन्त्रमय, भक्तसंघ से समन्वित, हरिहरात्मज, सर्वपापविनाशक, सर्वलोकहितेरत!

जो भक्तिभाव से इस अष्टक का पाठ करता है, उसे शास्ता का प्रसाद और सर्वसिद्धि प्राप्त होती है। मण्डलव्रत काल में पाठ करने से समस्त सम्पदा मिलती है।
benefits: Invokes the blessings of Lord Ayyappa, son of Shiva and Vishnu (Mohini)
Especially powerful during the Mandala Kalam (41-day vrat period)
Traditionally recited for removal of all sins and grants spiritual purification
Traditionally recited for fulfillment of the desire to visit sabarimala temple
Invokes protection, courage, and dharmic strength
Powerful during Makara Sankranti and Makara Jyoti darshan period
description: The Ayyappa Ashtakam, known in Sanskrit as अयप्पा अष्टकम्, is an eight-verse devotional hymn in praise of Lord Ayyappa. Rooted in Dharma Shastra Sangrah, this composition carries deep spiritual significance. Regular recitation is believed to destroy all sins and grant spiritual purification, invoke the blessings of Lord Ayyappa, son of Shiva and Vishnu (Mohini), and fulfill the desire to visit Sabarimala temple. It is especially recommended on Saturday (Shanivar) for maximum spiritual benefit. With 8 verses and a reading time of about 8 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Ayyappa Ashtakam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Ayyappa Ashtakam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Eight verses to Lord Ayyappa of Sabarimala for protection, devotion, and spiritual accomplishment.
alsoKnownAs: Eight Verses to Lord Ayyappa of Sabarimala

seoFaqs.question: What is the Ayyappa Ashtakam and what does it praise?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ayyappa Ashtakam (अयप्पा अष्टकम्) is an eight-verse devotional hymn in praise of Lord Ayyappa Swamy — Hari-Hara-Atmaja, the divine son of Lord Vishnu (in the form of Mohini) and Lord Shiva, and the presiding deity of the renowned Sabarimala temple in the Western Ghats of Kerala. The hymn opens with a dhyana shloka invoking Mahishi-suta — the slayer of the demoness Mahishi — followed by eight verses naming Ayyappa as Manikantha (jewel-throated), Maha-vira (great hero), Dharma-shasta (lord of dharma), Shabari-giri-vasa (dweller of Shabari hill), the deliverer of the meaning of Tattvam-asi, the eternal brahmachari, the lord of Patinettam-padi (the eighteen sacred steps) and the ever-radiant Makara-jyothi. (Sources: Dharma Shastra Sangrah; Ayyappa upasana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is Lord Ayyappa and why is he called Hari-Hara-Atmaja?
seoFaqs.answer: Lord Ayyappa, also called Manikantha, Dharma Shasta and Sabarimala Swamy, is revered in Kerala and across South India as Hari-Hara-Atmaja — 'the son of both Hari (Vishnu) and Hara (Shiva).' According to the puranic narrative, when Lord Vishnu assumed the enchanting form of Mohini to recover the Amrita from the asuras during Samudra Manthana, Lord Shiva was drawn to that form, and from their divine union was born Ayyappa — incarnated specifically to slay the demoness Mahishi (sister of Mahishasura), who could only be killed by one born of two male deities. He grew up as the foster-son of King Rajashekhara of Pandalam (Panthala-raja-palitam), assumed his divine form on the Shabari hill, and remains there as a brahmachari deity to this day. (Sources: Bhutanatha Upakhyana; Ayyappa Sahasranama tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the significance of the Mandala Vrata mentioned in this hymn?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Ayyappa Ashtakam declares that recitation of the hymn during the Mandala-vrata-kala (the 41-day period of austerities) bestows sarva-sampada (all prosperity) and the boon of Shabari-giri-darshana (vision of the Sabarimala lord). The Mandala Vrata begins on the first day of the Malayalam month of Vrishchika (mid-November) and concludes on Mandala-puja day (around late December), spanning 41 days. During this period, Ayyappa devotees called Ayyappa-swamis wear the black mala and dhoti, abstain from all worldly pleasures, observe celibacy, eat only sattvic food, recite the 108 Saranam mantras and stotras like this Ashtakam, and undertake the pilgrimage to Sabarimala carrying the Irumudi-kettu. The Ashtakam is recited daily as part of nityapaath. (Sources: Ayyappa Vrata paddhati; Sabarimala traditional practice.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of 'Swamiye Sharanam Ayyappa'?
seoFaqs.answer: The mantra Swamiye Sharanam Ayyappa — explicitly invoked in verse 7 of the Ashtakam (Sharana-mantra-mayam Devam Svamiye Sharanam-ityapi) — is the cardinal saranam mantra of the entire Ayyappa tradition. Swamiye: 'O my Lord'; Sharanam: 'I take refuge'; Ayyappa: 'in you, Ayyappa.' Together: 'O Lord Ayyappa, I take refuge in you.' This single phrase, repeated millions of times by pilgrims trekking up the Shabari hill, is considered to encompass the entire bhakti-yoga of Ayyappa upasana — total surrender to Hari-Hara-Atmaja for protection, guidance and ultimate liberation. The Ashtakam embeds this saranam mantra within its fabric to constantly remind the sadhaka of the path of sharanagati. (Sources: Ayyappa Saranam tradition; Sabarimala upasana.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Tattvamasi at Sabarimala in verse 5?
seoFaqs.answer: Verse 5 — Nilimala-nivasam tam Tattvam-asi-artha-bodhakam, Brahmacharya-vrata-stham tam Ayyappam pranamamyaham — calls Lord Ayyappa the bodhaka (revealer) of the meaning of the Mahavakya Tattvam-asi ('That thou art'). The famous golden inscription Tattvamasi adorns the entrance of the Sabarimala sannidhanam, declaring to every pilgrim that the Lord he has come to see is none other than his own innermost Self. After 41 days of Vrata, after climbing the eighteen sacred Patinettam-padi (each step representing a layer of ego, kama, krodha, lobha, etc.), the devotee finally beholds Ayyappa — and reads above the Lord the message that the Self he has been seeking outside has been within all along. The Ashtakam therefore frames Ayyappa not only as a bhakti deity but as the supreme jnana-guru. (Sources: Sabarimala temple tradition; Advaita-Ayyappa interpretation.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how should the Ayyappa Ashtakam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Ayyappa Ashtakam is most traditionally recited daily during the 41-day Mandala Vrata, ideally at Brahma muhurta after bath, wearing the black mala, before a picture of Ayyappa with a lamp lit in pure ghee. It is also auspicious throughout the year on Saturdays (Shanivar — Ayyappa's day), on Makara Sankranti (when the Makara-jyothi appears at Ponnambalamedu), on every Sunday of the Malayalam month of Dhanu, on the first of every Malayalam month, on Mandala Puja day and Vishu. Devotees who cannot travel to Sabarimala recite it as a substitute darshana, along with Harivarasanam at night. The 108-time chanting on Saturdays is held especially powerful for fulfilment of vrata-sankalpa. (Sources: Ayyappa upasana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the benefits of reciting the Ayyappa Ashtakam?
seoFaqs.answer: The phalashruti of the Ayyappa Ashtakam itself promises: Shastuh prasadam-aapnoti — receipt of the grace of Dharma-Shasta; sarva-siddhim cha vindati — attainment of all spiritual and worldly siddhis; sarva-sampadah during Mandala-vrata-paatha — every prosperity for those who recite during the 41-day vrata; and Shabari-giri-darsham na atra samshayah — guaranteed vision of the Sabarimala Lord without doubt. Traditional acharyas additionally extol: removal of all sins (sarva-papa-vinashanam), cultivation of brahmacharya and dharmic strength, protection on the difficult forest pilgrimage path, courage in life's battles, awakening of viveka through the Tattvam-asi teaching, and ultimately moksha — for Ayyappa is recognised as both the bhakti-deva and the jnana-guru. (Sources: Ayyappa Ashtakam Phalashruti; Sabarimala tradition.)
